business_domain: E-commerce Data Warehousing and Customer Analytics

key_entities.name: customers
key_entities.description: The primary customer dimension table, aggregating data from staging customers, orders, and payments.

key_entities.name: orders
key_entities.description: The final orders table representing sales transactions, dependent on customer data.

key_entities.name: stg_payments
key_entities.description: A staging layer for raw payment transactions before they are aggregated into customer metrics.

relationships: Raw source tables (raw_customers, raw_orders, raw_payments) feed into staging models (stg_).
The 'customers' mart is derived from the union of staging customers, orders, and payments models.
The 'orders' mart consumes the 'customers' mart as a dependency for downstream modeling.
Airflow DAGs (dbt_advanced_utility) import and utilize 'dbt_dag_parser.py' to orchestrate dbt model execution.

risks.critical_paths: The 'include\dbt_dag_parser.py' module is a single point of failure with high complexity (13.0) and high Pagerank (0.38), indicating it is the core logic for the orchestration layer.
risks.recommendations: Investigate 'dags\dbt_advanced.py' which is flagged as a dead code candidate. Refactor 'dbt_dag_parser.py' to reduce complexity and ensure it has high test coverage.

architecture: A dbt-based transformation pipeline orchestrated by Apache Airflow. The system follows a standard Medallion-style staging/marts architecture where raw data is cleaned in staging models and aggregated into business entities in the marts layer. Orchestration is managed via Python-based DAGs that use a centralized utility to parse dbt manifests and generate tasks.
